name: Shawthab
title: The Loyal Servant
shortDescription: A brave Shia warrior and ally of Abis who had benefited from the presence of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) in Kufa.
fullStory:
Shawthab ibn Abdullah al-Hamdani al-Shakiri was an outstanding and brave Shia man in Kufa. He was one of those who had experienced the presence of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) and heard many hadiths from him. He was also a close ally and companion of 'Abis ibn Abi Shabib'.

When Imam Hussain's (AS) caravan was besieged in Karbala, Shawthab, along with Abis, left Kufa and reached the Imam. On the day of Ashura, before Abis went to the battlefield, he turned to Shawthab and asked: 'O Shawthab, what is in your heart and what do you intend to do?'

Shawthab, with certainty full of faith, replied: 'What will I do? I will fight alongside you to defend the son of the Prophet's daughter until I am killed'. Abis, very pleased with this loyalty, told him: 'I expected nothing less from you. So go to the battlefield before me so that I may seek your reward from God'.

Shawthab first went to Imam Hussain (AS), greeted him, and bid him farewell. Then, with unparalleled bravery, he entered the battlefield and attacked the massive Kufan army. Despite the inequality in numbers, he put up a remarkable fight and killed several enemies.

Finally, after a heroic battle, he too was surrounded by the enemy and attained the lofty status of martyrdom through sword and spear blows. His loyalty to Wilayah and his companionship with Abis until his last breath recorded his name among the purest martyrs of Karbala.
quoteArabic: أُقاتِلُ مَعَکَ دُونَ ابْنِ بِنْتِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ حَتّى أُقْتَلَ
quote: I will fight alongside you to defend the son of the Messenger of God's daughter until I am killed.
